articles.title: Learner driver charged over Supanova expo death at Melbourne showgrounds
articles.description: Werribee man, 33, was charged with dangerous driving causing death and culpable driving after a man was struck and killed at Comic Con event Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A learner driver has been charged with killing a pedestrian after a car ploughed into a group outside a popular comic convention. A 20-year-old man died and another man the same age was seriously injured when a Toyota sedan mounted the kerb outside Melbourne Showgrounds in Ascot Vale just before 5pm on Saturday. articles.title: PNP instructor in custody for allegedly raping female trainee twice
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines – A 41-year-old police training instructor has been placed under restrictive custody, as the Philippine National Police (PNP) launched investigations after the instructor was accused of raping a female trainee twice inside a Cordillera facility earlier this year. PNP chief Gen. Jose Melencio C. Nartatez Jr., in a statement on Sunday, said the instructor has been disarmed and transferred to the PNP training headquarters in Laguna.Both criminal and administrative cases are now being prepared. PNP trainee assaulted twice Based on initial findings, the 23-year-old complainant filed a formal complaint on April 13, alleging she was sexually assaulted twice. [...]... Keep on reading: PNP instructor in custody for allegedly raping female trainee twice

articles.title: Austria recalls baby food jars in rat poison scare
articles.description: VIENNA — A supplier of baby food jars in Austria is recalling a line after discovering that rat poison may have been introduced through tampering. The HiPP brand said it was recalling its “entire line of baby food jars sold at SPAR Austria” as “it cannot be ruled out that a hazardous substance was introduced into the ‘HiPP Carrot/Potato 190 grams’ product through tampering, and consumption could be fatal”. Austria’s agency for food protection said police were investigating and that the rat poison may have been introduced as part of an extortion scheme. READ: FDA oversees disposal of infant formula [...]... Keep on reading: Austria recalls baby food jars in rat poison scare
